channel-type.label: Send Notification
channel-type.description: Send a notification message to this client. The message is displayed as an overlay on the client's screen.

channel-type.label: Playing Item ID
channel-type.description: Unique identifier of the item currently playing.

channel-type.label: Playing Item Name
channel-type.description: Name (title) of the item currently playing.

channel-type.label: Series Name
channel-type.description: Series name for the currently playing item. Present only when the item is an episode.

channel-type.label: Season Name
channel-type.description: Season name for the currently playing item. Present only when the item is an episode.

channel-type.label: Season Number
channel-type.description: Season number of the currently playing episode (integer). Present only when item is an episode.

channel-type.label: Episode Number
channel-type.description: Episode number of the currently playing episode (integer). Present only when item is an episode.

channel-type.label: Genres
channel-type.description: Comma-separated list of genres for the currently playing item.

channel-type.label: Item Type
channel-type.description: Media type of the currently playing item (e.g. Movie, Episode, Music).

channel-type.label: Playback Percentage
channel-type.description:
Current playback position as a percentage (0–100%).

Read the current position or send a percentage to seek. Values outside the 0–100 range are ignored.

channel-type.label: Playback Position
channel-type.description:
Current playback position.

Read the current position or send a value (in seconds) to seek to that timestamp.

channel-type.label: Total Duration
channel-type.description: Total duration of the currently playing item. Read-only.

channel-type.label: Play by Terms
channel-type.description: Play media matching the given search terms (title, series, or other metadata).

channel-type.label: Play Next by Terms
channel-type.description: Add the first media matching the terms as the next item in the playback queue.

channel-type.label: Play Last by Terms
channel-type.description: Add the first media matching the terms to the end of the playback queue.

channel-type.label: Browse by Terms
channel-type.description: Query the server for media matching the given search terms.

channel-type.label: Play by ID
channel-type.description: Play a media item identified by the given server ID.

channel-type.label: Play Next by ID
channel-type.description: Add the specified media ID as the next item in the playback queue.

channel-type.label: Play Last by ID
channel-type.description: Add the specified media ID to the end of the playback queue.

channel-type.label: Browse by ID
channel-type.description: Retrieve metadata or browse media based on the specified server ID.

channel-type.label: Stop
channel-type.description:
Stop playback control.

Send ON to stop playback. This ends playback and may reset the playback position depending on the client.

Note: Use the media-control channel for play, pause, next, previous, fast-forward, and rewind commands.

channel-type.label: Shuffle
channel-type.description:
Toggle shuffle mode for the playback queue.

ON: Items in the queue will play in random order.
OFF: Items in the queue will play in sequential order.

channel-type.label: Repeat Mode
channel-type.description:
Set the repeat mode for playback.

  • off: Play queue once and stop
  • one: Repeat current item continuously
  • all: Repeat entire queue continuously

channel-type.label: Streaming Quality
channel-type.description:
Set the maximum streaming bitrate to control video quality.

Higher bitrates provide better quality but require more bandwidth:

  • 140000 (140 Kbps): Low quality, minimal bandwidth
  • 500000 (500 Kbps): Medium quality, suitable for mobile
  • 1500000 (1.5 Mbps): High quality, SD streaming
  • 3000000 (3 Mbps): Very high quality, HD streaming
  • 8000000 (8 Mbps): Ultra quality, Full HD streaming

channel-type.label: Audio Track
channel-type.description:
Select the audio track for the current media item.

Use the zero-based stream index to switch between available audio tracks (e.g., different languages or audio formats).

Example: 0 for first track, 1 for second track, etc.

channel-type.label: Subtitle
channel-type.description:
Select or disable the subtitle track for the current media item.

Use the stream index to switch between available subtitle tracks:

  • -1: Disable subtitles
  • 0: First subtitle track
  • 1: Second subtitle track
  • etc.

channel-type.label: Now Playing Image
channel-type.description:
Cover art image for the currently playing item.

The image is fetched from the Jellyfin server and published as binary data. The image type (Primary, Backdrop, etc.) and maximum width are configurable per client.
